basics.name: S.M. Obaydur Rahman
basics.label: Software Engineer & ML Researcher
basics.summary: Software Engineer with a research orientation in NLP, deep learning, and applied machine intelligence. CSE graduate of Shahjalal University of Science and Technology (SUST), currently working at Mutual Trust Bank PLC. Actively seeking PhD opportunities in NLP, low-resource language AI, and ML for sustainable development.

work.name: Mutual Trust Bank PLC
work.position: Software Engineer — Satellite & Digital Ecosystem Development Unit
highlights: Developing and maintaining software systems within the Satellite & Digital Ecosystem Development Unit under the Digital Banking Division.
Contributing to fintech infrastructure modernization and AI-assisted digital banking workflows.
Working at the intersection of enterprise software engineering and emerging financial technology.

work.name: V Shipping BD
work.summary: End-to-end development of logistics and shipping management systems.
highlights: Architected and built full-stack web applications for global shipping operations using Next.js and Node.js.
Designed and optimized relational database schemas for large-scale logistics data.
Collaborated across cross-functional teams to deliver reliable, production-grade software under tight deadlines.

work.name: Dana Fintech Ltd
work.summary: Backend engineering for a fintech platform serving micro-finance and digital lending.
highlights: Developed and maintained RESTful API services in PHP and CodeIgniter for a digital lending platform.
Worked with Oracle database systems, including RMAN backup strategies and Toad-based query optimization.
Contributed to reducing backend latency through systematic query profiling and index optimization.

education.institution: Shahjalal University of Science and Technology (SUST)
education.studyType: B.Sc. in Computer Science and Engineering
highlights: Thesis: *Sentiment Analysis of Social Media Comments on COVID-19 Post-Vaccination in Bangladesh* — applied classical ML, ensemble, and deep learning models to a manually annotated Bengali dataset.
Supervisor: Mohammad Abdullah Al Mumin, PhD, Professor, Dept. of CSE, SUST.

publications.name: Advanced Hybrid Deep Learning Models for Solar Energy Holding Capacity Prediction in Bangladesh
publications.summary: Proposed a hybrid stacking ensemble framework combining Random Forest, XGBoost, Gradient Boosting, and AdaBoost with an MLP meta-learner to forecast solar energy holding capacity across districts of Bangladesh. The model integrates diverse climatic and environmental indicators to support renewable energy planning and policy-making.
publications.authors: Aminul Islam, S.M. Obaydur Rahman, Akbar Hossin, Rubaia Sharika

publications.name: Sentiment Analysis of Social Media Comments on COVID-19 Post-Vaccination in Bangladesh
publications.publisher: Undergraduate Thesis, Dept. of CSE, SUST
publications.summary: Investigated public sentiment toward COVID-19 vaccination in Bangladesh using a manually annotated Bengali dataset of 3,807 social media records. Applied and benchmarked classical ML classifiers, ensemble methods, and deep learning models (RNN, LSTM, GRU) with multiple feature extraction strategies including TF-IDF, N-grams, BoW, and Word2Vec.

publications.name: In silico investigations on curcuminoids from Curcuma longa as positive regulators of the Wnt/β-catenin signaling pathway in wound healing
publications.publisher: Egyptian Journal of Medical Human Genetics, Vol. 22, Issue 65
publications.summary: Co-authored a computational biology study applying molecular docking and in-silico predictive modeling to investigate curcuminoids from Curcuma longa as potential activators of key proteins in the Wnt/β-catenin wound-healing pathway. Published in a Springer Nature journal; 15+ citations.
publications.authors: R.A.I. Reshad, S. Alam, H.B. Raihan, K.N. Meem, F. Rahman, F. Zahid, M.I. Rafid, S.M. Obaydur Rahman, S. Omit, M.H. Ali

projects.name: COVID-19 Post-Vaccination Sentiment Analyzer
projects.summary: End-to-end ML pipeline for Bengali social media sentiment analysis on COVID-19 vaccination discourse. Built a manually annotated dataset of 3,807 records from Facebook, Twitter, and Instagram, and benchmarked nine ML/DL models across four feature extraction strategies.
highlights: Stack: Python, Flask (API), Next.js (Frontend), TensorFlow, scikit-learn
Models: NB, SVM, LR, Decision Tree, RF, AdaBoost, Gradient Boosting, RNN, LSTM, GRU
Features: TF-IDF, N-grams, Bag-of-Words, Word2Vec (Gensim, size 3000)

projects.name: Solar Energy Holding Capacity Forecaster
projects.summary: Hybrid stacking ensemble model for predicting solar energy holding capacity across Bangladesh districts. Base learners (RF, XGBoost, GradientBoosting, AdaBoost) feed into an MLP meta-learner, integrating environmental, climatic, and socio-economic features.
highlights: Dataset: Kaggle-sourced district-level Bangladesh solar data with engineered features

projects.name: WiFi-Talk — LAN Chat via WebSocket
projects.summary: Real-time LAN chat application built to demonstrate WebSocket communication. Implemented a Flutter mobile client and a Node.js WebSocket server, applying provider pattern, web_socket_channel, and UUID packages.
highlights: Stack: Flutter, Dart, Node.js (ws npm package)
Demonstrates: WebSocket protocol, Flutter provider state management, LAN networking
